LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-23-2020 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NABEEL ALVIE AND KIRAN ALVIE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF AGREES THAT "COUNTS" TWO AND THREE OF HIS PREMISES LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION ARE UNAVAILING AND THAT NEGLIGENCE PER SE IS AN EVIDENTIARY PRESUMPTION, NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. THUS, DEFENDANTS ARE GRANTED SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THOSE POINTS. THE MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: RICHARD B. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Oct-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JASON R. DOUCETTE, Esq. of McLachlan LAW, APC (Tel: 310-954-8270) for Plaintiff Adam Hadjinian;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NABEEL ALVIE AND KIRAN ALVIE'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Adam Hadjinian fell and severely broke his ankle on stairs at a dwelling rented from defendants Nabeel and Kiran Alvie. As defendants note, the complaint's causes of action for premises liability, negligence and negligence per se "are all based upon negligence principles."

Defendants base their summary judgment/adjudication motion on assertions that there are no triable issue of material facts "as to the existence of an unreasonably dangerous condition" and their knowledge of the condition. (Memo. 4:9-13.) I disagree. A lay person viewing the steps - much less a professional - could readily see that they lack a handrail and are dangerously steep and shallow, and defendants themselves lived in the dwelling for years. (Kiran Alvie Dec. Ex. A (photo of steps); SSO 6, 9, 12.) Expert testimony that the steps were dangerous and violated state and local codes is icing on the cake. (Perry Dec.)

Plaintiff agrees that "counts" two and three of his premises liability cause of action are unavailing and that negligence per se is an evidentiary presumption, not a cause of action. Thus, defendants are granted summary adjudication on those points. The motion is otherwise denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
